📊 ADVANCED AGENT MARKETING - ANÁLISE DE ANÚNCIOS
[01:12] So first of all, one of the first ads that we see looks to be here we go for Spanish life insurance agents. Very interesting niche.
[01:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Spanish Life Insurance Agents Ad"
NÚMEROS: 10 variantes de imagem, 6 meses, 8-10 appointments por dia
VISUAL: Múltiplas variações de imagem com mesmo estilo, palavra censurada com bip, claim de renda com timeframe
[01:25] It also says that this ad has multiple variants. Every variant appears to be an image. I would assume that this specific style of creative has worked tremendously well for you if you've used the exact same style of image with subtle variations in the copy on it 10 different times.
[01:42] We historically like to use a mix of images, gifts, and videos, mainly videos, inside of our dynamic creative adsets. We find that they work tremendously well.
[01:52] But again, maybe you've already done that. Maybe you spent a lot of money and figured out that this specific set of images works best for you.
[02:01] Second thing that I noticed, the little bad word that's bleeped out. That's a big no no inside of compliance on Facebook and almost every ad channel for that matter.
[02:12] Tik Tok, I've noticed you can get away with it a little more, but not on Facebook. Good old Facebook and Instagram requires you to be extremely compliant.
[02:22] Saying any kind of vulgar language is obviously going to get you disabled, or I should say disapproved and potentially disabled. Bleeping out words like what you've chosen to do here. Big risk.
[02:35] I can tell right away that you're spending literally next to no money. Definitely sub thousand dollars a day on a campaign that has this. This is non-compliant.

[03:27] There's one particular thing that I would like to point out, which is this headline right here. How to book 8 to 10 life insurance appointments per day. That is a really good claim. That's compliant. It's also conclusive.
[03:42] What I mean by that is somebody can read that type of claim and then make a conclusion where they're like, I could likely make that much money per month or per year or however they think with it, per day as an example.
[03:57] All just based on that claim without having to use actual income numbers associated with it. Very important.

[04:53] Very low. It's more probable that it's just going to immediately play the video.

[05:31] So, guarantees can also be a big turnoff to a more sophisticated buyer that you're otherwise trying to convert. Keep that in mind because that's really going to fuck you if you're trying to attract a more financially qualified individual.
[05:45] And again, what you can see here on almost every single one of these is that that is the headline being used. To be fair to you, you're running dynamic creative on this one.
[05:57] Looks like the exact same ad video on almost every single one of these. Yeah. So, long story short, you did a really good job with having a very wide range of content for your hammer them strategy.
[06:10] But it looks to be that you did a very poor job on using videos inside of your actual direct response ads. There were a few to be fair.
[06:20] But I'd love to see a lot more videos in your direct response ads. And in addition to that, I would also love to see you be compliant so when you actually start spending some real money, you do not get disabled.
[06:34] Because at the end of the day, that's not going to be very fun in the instance that that occurs.
[06:41] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Landing Page - Attention Life Insurance Agents"
NÚMEROS: $20K/mês claim, $53K→$312K, 100K/mês em 85 dias
VISUAL: Headline não-compliante, multiple income claims, cherry-picked testimonials sem disclaimer
[06:43] Okay. So, here's the funnel. Here's where people get driven to. Attention life insurance agents, we'll get you qualified leads and appointments. And if you don't sell at least 20K a month, you don't pay.
[06:58] Again, super non-compliant. What I would rather see as the main claim is the get the eight to 10 pre-booked appointments per day.
[07:08] So imagine if the headline was we'll get you 8 to 10 qualified appointments per day or you don't pay like something like that, you know.
[07:18] And again, you don't even necessarily have to do a guarantee and do the you don't pay part. You could just say, "We'll get you 8 to 10 pre-booked appointments that are qualified and likely to buy per day for your life insurance business."
[07:33] You know, something like that. Again, when you look right here, super non-compliant. You clearly know nothing about the FTC and how it works.
[07:44] And again, this is a huge indication that you're spending no money. When you start making some real money, you will give a deep fuck about the FTC. That's why I keep bringing it up.
[07:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "FTC Compliance Warning - Cherry-picked Testimonials"
NÚMEROS: $53K→$312K claim, 100K/mês, 85 dias
VISUAL: Exemplo de testimonial não-compliante, falta de earnings disclaimer, violação de substantiation rules
[07:58] The FTC will fuck you so hard here. This is going to be evidence used in your trial of your downfall. My friend, you can't cherrypick a testimonial and not have a very clear disclaimer written in the same size text that it is not the average result.
[08:15] Give you a great example of that just so you can see that I practice what I preach and you know I'm not full of shit. Let's use the inner circle that you're a part of as a good example of this.
[08:26] If we go to my inner circle page, first of all, one of the very first giant fucking text disclaimers that you see is me saying just as I mentioned in the exact same font size and it is the first section that somebody sees after the main VSSL before I go into all the testimonials.
[08:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inner Circle Earnings Disclaimer Example"
NÚMEROS: Tamanho de fonte igual para disclaimer
VISUAL: Disclaimer massivo e visível, mesmo tamanho de texto, posicionamento estratégico antes dos testimonials
[08:48] So these testimonials, they do actually represent both an above average, below average, and average person inside of the group. However, I can't highlight all of these individuals like this unless I have a massive earnings disclaimer associated with it.
[09:05] So, keep that in mind because when you cherrypick a testimonial like this and you say, "Oh, we spent 53 grand, flipped it into 312 grand, we can get you to 100K a month in 85 days."
[09:18] Those are implied claims. And unless that is the literal average result that you generate for every single person that does business with you, that's a cherrypicked testimonial.
[09:30] And you cannot do that without an extremely clear disclaimer that that's the case and that it's cherrypicked.
[09:38] The only testimonials that you can technically use are substantiated testimonials, which again reflect true averages for the results that people are going to generate within your community or your product or whatever.

NÚMEROS: $20K/mês average, range de resultados
VISUAL: Necessidade de mostrar range (pior, médio, melhor), não apenas cherry-pick, apelo a demografia mais ampla
[17:20] Yeah. And the main thing I want to make clear here, like when I say it sounds like you're using unsubstantiated claims by talking about this 20k a month number, the person who doesn't necessarily believe that they're above average, which is going to be a lot of your buyers, believe it or not.
[17:38] They'd rather hear you also talk about like how bad it is. Like you need to say a range as an example. You want to be like, look, our average person does this, our worst people do this, and our best people do this.
[17:52] That's a better way to articulate yourself when you're going through that specific set of points because it comes off as a lot more believable because it is.
[18:02] Don't just cherrypick the specific things that you think people want is the outcome. Give the range because then it appeals to the broadest demographic.
[18:13] Lead vendors that could go under any point and leave you and your business high and dry or without paying expensive marketing company retainers. Once we set this up for you, you own the system outright.
[18:26] It's a onetime payment done for you and you never have to buy leads again and you can enjoy much larger profits and keep way more money when you sell insurance. Here's what I'm going to cover in this video. First, I'm going to talk about expected conversions and results.

[24:14] Again, dude. Like each one of these could easily be paired with a video, you know, like showcasing you setting up the leads and then being like, "Let's check in a day later and see how everything works."
[24:27] And like 24 hours, you show how much was spent. You show how many leads have been generated and you show like the quality of the lead.
[24:36] Like maybe bleep out the person's information with a little blur box, but showcase some of the conditional logic questions that I'm sure you're using on those.

[26:54] And then they just get fucking stuck there because there's a massive difference in the tactics that you have to implement.
[27:03] All this being an example of it that you have to like make more sophisticated. You have to like change the tactics. You know, you're in a group full of people that are consistently telling you, "Hey, I took this action that Jeremy did and it worked tremendously well."

[1:22:12] Does Australia have an FTC equivalent? So, does Australia have an FTC equivalent? Yes, Australia has an equivalent to the US Federal Trade Commission called the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission, the ACCC.
[1:22:30] The ACCC is responsible for enforcing Australia's competition and consumer protection laws. Yes, there's a whole bunch of consumer protection laws.
[1:22:42] And let's find out. Can you make income claims in ads in Australia? Yeah, here you go. No, in Australia, you generally cannot make explicit income claims in advertisements without being able to fully substantiate them.
[1:22:58] So if the true average to be clear substantiating means the true average is that 3 to 5k that you consistently talk about. Okay.
[1:23:10] So long story short you have a whole web page here from the ACCC in your Australian version of the FTC that clearly lays out all the different rules that you must follow.
[1:23:24] And it looks like they're just a spitting image of the FTC's version of all these same rules.
[1:23:32] So, long story short, there's probably an ACCC version of a consultant that you can likely hire or maybe even you upload this exact rule set into ChatGPT and you run your current ad script against it to get the same outcome that I'm sitting here telling you about right now.

[1:25:20] And every video that we talk about has an earnings disclaimer. And as I already showed earlier in this video, when you go to our inner circle landing page, giant fucking earnings disclaimer, too. Okay.
[1:25:34] Anyway, yeah, shorter variations on the actual ad copy would be nice to see. And then just clean up the headline, that second headline specifically.

[1:33:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Event Logistics Best Practices"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Hotels recomendados, airports mais próximos (commercial + private), restaurantes, opções para famílias, travel logistics
[1:33:04] You know, a lot of the things that are left unaddressed here is like the hotels that people can stay at. How—I'll give you a good one.
[1:33:16] One of my buddies invited me to something out in Palm Springs. And when I immediately looked up flights from like Miami to Palm Springs, it looked like a huge fucking pain in the ass.
[1:33:30] Like instantaneously, I was like, "Okay, so there's no direct flight from Miami to Palm Springs. How the fuck do I get there? What's the easiest way?"
[1:33:42] Then I had to start doing a bunch of travel math, you know, which was super whack. I had to think like, do I go from Miami to LAX and then do I take like a Sprinter van from LAX to Palm Springs?
[1:33:58] Is there a closer airport to Palm Springs that does have a direct flight I'm just unaware of? Like what's the fucking best way to do this?
[1:34:10] You know, and again, just in this example, like a simple email articulating to me, what's the easiest way to get there? What's the easiest airport?
[1:34:22] If you notice in like every single time that we do a mastermind, when we put the mastermind details, we put the closest commercial airport, the closest private airport.
[1:34:36] And when we had the farm mansion as an example, we even had an airstrip in the backyard that people could use if they coordinated with us ahead of time.
[1:34:48] So anyway, be mindful of all that, helping people understand what they genuinely want to know. You know, what is there to do? What are the nicest hotels in the area?
[1:35:00] Are there nice restaurants that you can recommend? These are things that also people care about when they're considering traveling to and from there.
[1:35:12] Some people bring their families when they go to these things. So, you also want to be mindful of telling people like, "Hey, if you're going to come with your wife and kids or your partner or somebody, here's the best hotels for those types of people."
[1:35:28] These are all things that you want to consider outside of just the, you know, content of the event, the people going to the event, all the testimonials from the event.
[1:35:42] Like, those are obviously all good emails to incorporate as well, but, you know, anyway, just keep all that in mind. Good stuff overall.

[1:36:26] Excited to do another one of these. We typically do them every week. Sometimes every other week depending on how many of you have added in material for us to review.
[1:36:40] And you'll find these in the weekly inner circle group call library in the instance that you are watching this in the telegram group chat.
[1:36:52] So, if you are in the weekly group call library, the link is below this video. If you are in the Telegram group watching this video, right above or right below this, you should see the link to the Google doc for this information as well.
[1:37:08] All right, cool. Talk soon, guys.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Advanced Agent Marketing: 8-10 appointments/dia, $20K-$100K/mês claims, $53K→$312K flip, 100K/mês em 85 dias, 6 meses timeframe
Just and Prob (Agent Attraction): 35 agent downline, $30K-$300K/mês online, 10 appointments em 7 dias, 12 appointments/dia, $40K/mês, $189K biggest month, $1.2M total revenue, $71K/dia recente
Cameron (Team POW): 3-5 fig withdrawals (compliant), até $200K prop funding, 2x playback speed
Jimmy (Moves Method): $67 low-ticket, 75% off, 14-day money back
James (E-commerce): 3-10K/mês em 90 dias, 2,000 Aussies, 1 hora/dia requirement
Brett (Four Rooms): 7-9 figure founders, over $1M em partnerships
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Compliance: Income claims com timeframes = violação massiva, uso de ranges ao invés de números específicos, substantiation obrigatória, "3-5 fig" como claim compliante
Modern Due Diligence: Full name + company name em emails, video assets para research, links para testimonials, trust badges, risk disclaimers
Email Marketing: Bait email ("sent from iPhone"), video chapters em VSLs, Gamma docs (mas com limitações mobile), playlist strategy para YouTube
Video Best Practices: Wistia com thumbnail branded, chapters para scrubbing, controls sempre disponíveis, 2x speed consideration, nunca autoplay
Ad Creative: Mix de images/gifs/videos, dynamic creative, Hammer Them campaign, in-market messaging, compliant hooks
Landing Pages: Headline + sub-headline + video + button, branding elevado, Trust Pilot widgets, proper sections
ESTRUTURA:
Fox Tag: Protege contra AI mas não contra human reviewers, manual reviews começam em $50K-$100K/mês ad spend
FTC/ACCC: Mesmas regras US e Australia, cherry-picked testimonials ilegais sem disclaimer, substantiation obrigatória
Scale Problems: $100K-$200K/mês = stuck point, compliance necessária antes de scale, rug pulls ao escalar com non-compliance
Demographic Sophistication: Emojis = poor people, branding = rich people, guarantees = turnoff para sophisticated buyers
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Ad Spend Thresholds: Sub $1K/dia = baby money, $50K-$100K/mês = manual review trigger
Revenue Estimates: Advanced Agent Marketing ~$100K-$200K/mês
Fluctuation Impact: 20% down em couple hundred K/mês = mais dramático que 20% em $100K/mês
ROI Examples: $1K spend → $14K return (14x ROI) mencionado
DURAÇÃO: 1:37:08 FORMATO: Weekly Inner Circle Review Call - Análise de Ads, Funnels e Marketing Automation
